page: Vultr voc-c-4c-8gb-150s-amd is an instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor. This server provides 4 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core on a shared CPU allocation model. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz and is equipped with 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It includes 8.0 GB of system memory, translating to 2.0 GB per core, and is bundled with 150 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. No GPU hardware is included. A single complimentary public IPv4 address is provided.

Benchmark data reveals a mixture of strong single-core capabilities and average multi-core performance. In stress-ng testing, single-core CPU performance is rated as strong (top 25%) with a score of 2934.18, while multi-core performance falls into the average category at 11664.13. Memory bandwidth tests show average results, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 534.72 GB/sec, and large block uncached read and write speeds at 73.06 GB/sec and 60.22 GB/sec respectively. Passmark CPU and memory scores also align with average performance tiers. However, certain multi-threaded and database operations show limitations; bzip3 multi-threaded decompression and Passmark database operations are both positioned in the weak (bottom 25%) tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU allocation model and 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offer a resource-dense configuration suitable for compute-focused workloads that do not require dedicated CPU threads. The presence of local NVMe SSD storage provides fast local read/write capabilities, though database operations and static web serving throughput (4.46 GB/sec) are benchmarked in the lower tier. This server is suited for single-threaded processing tasks, basic containerized applications, and lightweight text generation workloads, where its strong single-core performance can be leveraged. It is less suited for heavy database deployments or highly parallelized multi-threaded decompression tasks.
description: Vultr voc-c-4c-8gb-150s-amd is an x86_64 server in the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 4 vCPUs on 4 physical cores with a shared CPU allocation. Powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor running at 2.0 GHz, it includes 8.0 GB of memory (2.0 GB per core) and 150 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmark results show strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics. However, the server exhibits weaker performance in database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded decompression. This configuration is best suited for compute-focused workloads that benefit from strong single-thread execution, such as lightweight application servers, basic container hosting, and simple text generation tasks, while avoiding heavy database or highly parallelized workloads.
og_description: Vultr voc-c-4c-8gb-150s-amd is an x86_64 server featuring 4 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and 150 GB NVMe SSD storage, offering strong single-core performance for compute-focused workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-c-4c-8gb-150s-amd features 4 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and 150 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core CPU performance.
tagline: An AMD Genoa-powered compute instance featuring 4 shared vCPUs, local NVMe SSD storage, and strong single-core processing capabilities.

8.0 GB system memory and 150 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Shared CPU allocation model with 2.0 GHz clock speed
Best suited for single-threaded tasks and lightweight application hosting
Weaker performance in database operations and multi-threaded decompression
